revision.comment: Restore image alignment, sizes and captions from legacy source (3030-30447)
revision.text:
{{Game | Name=Hover! | Guid=3030-30447 | Deck=A hovercraft "capture the flag"-style game that was included in the CD-ROM version of the Windows 95 operating system. It later received an official port to modern web browsers. | ReleaseDate=1995-08-31 | ReleaseDateType=Month | Characters= | Concepts=Concepts/Built_In_Game,Concepts/Capture_the_Flag,Concepts/First_Person_Perspective,Concepts/Power_Down,Concepts/Single_Word_Titles | Developers=Companies/Microsoft_Corporation | Franchises= | Genres=Genres/Action,Genres/Driving_Racing | Locations= | Objects= | Platforms=Platforms/Browser,Platforms/PC | Publishers=Companies/Microsoft_Corporation | Games=Games/Locus,Games/Necrodome,Games/Radix_Beyond_the_Void,Games/Rapid_Assault,Games/SlamScape,Games/Tunnel_B1,Games/Zone_Raiders | Themes=Themes/Sci_Fi | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/0/5303/1303028-hover.png }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"1303028-hover.png","path":"0\/5303\/","mime":"image\/png","sizes":"scale_medium,scale_super,square_mini,square_medium,square_small,screen_medium,square_avatar,square_tiny,scale_small,scale_large,scale_avatar,scale_tiny,screen_kubrick","caption":"Box art"},"background":[]}' />
==Overview== Hover! (also known as ''Microsoft Hover!'', and not to be confused with the 2017 game [[Games/Hover|Hover]]) is a sci-fi first-person hovercraft-racing game developed and released by [[Companies/Microsoft_Corporation|Microsoft]] for [https://www.giantbomb.com/pc/3045-94 Windows PCs] in August 1995, [[Concepts/Built_In_Game|included as a bonus in]] the installation disc for the CD-ROM version of the [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Windows_95 Windows 95] operating system.
In Hover!, players are put into the cockpit of the "Hover 950" hovercraft as they navigate an open maze to collect all the blue flags before their opponent drones collect all the red flags. Along the way, they can collect multiple power-ups (including speed boosts and shields) and must be careful to navigate around hazards (in the form of floor "pads" that either trap the hovercraft, send the hovercraft flying in one direction, or returning a flag from the hovercraft's possession).
The game includes three themed mazes (one set in a medieval castle, one set in a neo-futuristic city, and one set in an underground sewer), with the game looping between each maze while adding additional flags and more and smarter drones.
Although the game did not receive any standalone release or sequel, it received an enhanced port in 2013 as a [[Platforms/Browser|browser]] game to showcase [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/WebGL WebGL] support in [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Internet_Explorer_11 Microsoft's Internet Explorer 11] web browser. This version features higher-definition graphics and online multiplayer support, was later released in app form for the Windows Store, and was later discontinued in 2019.
==Gameplay== Along with standard [[Concepts/Tank_Controls|tank controls]] (in which players control their forwards and backwards momentum and can turn in either direction), players have access to three abilities (which are replenished with power-ups):
* '''Jump''' (defaults to 'A') - Propels the player upwards, allowing them to reach higher platforms and avoid hazards. * '''Wall''' (defaults to 'S') - Deploys a temporary wall behind the player. * '''Cloak''' (default to 'D') - Cloaks the player from enemy drones.
==Pods== Scattered around the arena are floating green "pods" that can be collected to gain one of eight effects:
{| class='wikitable gb-table-natural' |- ! Object||Effect |- | Jump||Adds a charge to the player's Jump ability. |- | Wall||Adds a charge to the player's Wall ability. |- | Cloak||Adds a charge to the player's Cloak ability. |- | Shield||Temporarily grants the player immunity from hazards and the Map Eraser and Speed Reduction pods. |- | Green Light||Temporarily boosts the player's maximum speed. |- | Red Light||Temporarily reduces the player's maximum speed. |- | Map Eraser||Removes a portion of the discovered map from the player's minimap. The player must return to that area to reveal that portion again. |- | Mystery||One of the seven above effects at random. |}

{{Game |Guid=ac8fb9c0-3b4b-411e-b87e-e875eea23ca5 |Name=Hunter: The Reckoning - Deathwish |Image=https://giantbomb.com/api/media/file/media--file--library_capsule_2x-03-27-2026.jpg |BackgroundImage=https://giantbomb.com/api/media/file/media--file--library_hero_2x-03-27-2026.jpg |ReleaseDateType=Quarter |QuarterPart=Q2 |QuarterYearPart=2027 |Concepts=Concepts/Digital Distribution, Concepts/First Person Perspective |Developers=Companies/Teyon |Franchises=Franchises/Hunter The Reckoning |Genres=Genres/First Person Shooter |Platforms=Platforms/PlayStation 5, Platforms/Xbox Series X S, Platforms/PC |Publishers=Companies/Nacon |Themes=Themes/Horror }}

{{Game |Guid=87dca545-60ba-42e8-8522-b11223290cc3 |Name=Husk Protocol |Image=https://giantbomb.com/api/media/file/media--file--library_capsule_2aaax-06-01-2026.jpg |BackgroundImage=https://giantbomb.com/api/media/file/media--file--library_hero_2xeee-06-01-2026.jpg |Deck=A roguelite deck-builder as humanity's last hope in a world dominated by machines. |ReleaseDateType=Full |Concepts=Concepts/Digital Distribution, Concepts/2D, Concepts/Steam, Concepts/Roguelike |Developers=Companies/OddSub |Genres=Genres/Strategy |Platforms=Platforms/PC |Publishers=Companies/Fireshine Games |Themes=Themes/Sci Fi }}

revision.comment: create page Hyakka Ryoran Master Samurai Chronicles games
revision.text: {{Game |Guid=9bd5367e-60f5-45de-a314-421b65307e4a |Name=Hyakka Ryoran Master Samurai Chronicles |Aliases=Master Samurai Chronicles |Image=Ryoransquare.jpg |Caption=Hyakka Ryoran Master Samurai Chronicles is a free-to-play turn-based idle role-playing browser game based on the light novel series Hyakka Ryoran Samurai Girls. |ReleaseDateType=Full |ReleaseDate=2026-07-03 |Concepts=Concepts/RPG Elements |Developers=Companies/CTW |Genres=Genres/MMORPG |Platforms=Platforms/Browser |Publishers=Companies/CTW |Games=Games/Chrome, Games/Firefox |Themes=Themes/Alternate Historical }} Hyakka Ryoran Master Samurai Chronicles is a free-to-play turn-based idle role-playing browser game based on the light novel series Hyakka Ryoran Samurai Girls by Akira Suzuki and Niθ. Players take the role of Yagyu Muneakira, a Buou Gakuen Juku student who can awaken Master Samurai powers in others, and lead a party of Master Samurai based on historical Japanese warlords. Auto-battle combat features a five-member party with tap-based Spirit intervention from Muneakira. Materials collected in idle combat feed into a Katana equipment system and cosmetic Skin loadouts. Thousands of stage floors provide long-term progression. The game launched July 3, 2026, operated by CTW, Inc. on the G123 platform, and runs in HTML5-capable browsers without download or installation.

{{Game |Guid=689504d8-74b0-4699-929c-83827e979be2 |Name=Idols of Ash |Image=Idols-of-ash-game-m318x318.jpg |Deck=Grapple, clamber and fall thousands of meters down into a dark and ancient place in this first-person horror climbing game. You are being hunted. |ReleaseDateType=Full |ReleaseDate=2026-04-09 |Concepts=Concepts/Climbing |Developers=Companies/Leafy Games LLC |Platforms=Platforms/PC |Publishers=Companies/Leafy Games LLC }} == Overview == Idols of Ash is a first-person climbing horror game developed and published by indie studio [[Companies/Leafy Games LLC|Leafy Games]]. Using a rope and grappling hook, the player must traverse downward into a large cavernous pit to visit ashen shrines while being chased by a monster.

{{Game | Name=J-Stars Victory Vs | Guid=3030-40839 | Deck=J-Stars Victory Vs is a fighting game celebrating Shonen Jump's longevity. | ReleaseDate=2014-03-19 | Characters=Characters/Akainu,Characters/Arale_Norimaki,Characters/Boa_Hancock,Characters/Bobobo_bo_Bo_bobo,Characters/Chitoge_Kirisaki,Characters/Don_Patch,Characters/Frieza,Characters/Goku,Characters/Gon_Freecss,Characters/Heihachi_Edajima,Characters/Hiei,Characters/Himura_Kenshin,Characters/Hisoka,Characters/Ichigo_Kurosaki,Characters/Jaguar,Characters/Jonathan_Joestar,Characters/Joseph_Joestar,Characters/Kagura_37668,Characters/Kenshiro,Characters/Killua_Zoldyck,Characters/Korosensei,Characters/Lala_Satalin_Deviluke,Characters/Lucky_Man,Characters/Madara_Uchiha,Characters/Makoto_Shishio,Characters/Momotaro_Tsurugi,Characters/Monkey_D_Luffy,Characters/Naruto_Uzumaki,Characters/Pegasus_Seiya,Characters/Portgas_D_Ace,Characters/Raoh,Characters/Reborn,Characters/Rukia_Kuchiki,Characters/Ryotsu_Kankichi,Characters/Sakata_Gintoki,Characters/Sasuke_Uchiha,Characters/Sosuke_Aizen,Characters/Tetsuya_Kuroko,Characters/Toriko_25128,Characters/Tsunayoshi_Sawada,Characters/Vegeta,Characters/Younger_Toguro,Characters/Yusuke_Urameshi | Concepts=Concepts/Anime_Fighter,Concepts/Anniversary_Edition,Concepts/Arena_Fighter,Concepts/Bot,Concepts/Character_Select_Screen,Concepts/Clownish_Combatant,Concepts/Countdown_Timer,Concepts/Crossover,Concepts/Digital_Distribution,Concepts/Dolby_Digital,Concepts/Dream_Match,Concepts/Elasticity,Concepts/Galick_Gun,Concepts/Health,Concepts/Hokuto_Shinken,Concepts/Iaido,Concepts/Kamehameha,Concepts/Licensed_Game,Concepts/Online,Concepts/Super_Saiyan,Concepts/Team_Up_Attack,Concepts/Tokyo_Game_Show_2013 | Developers=Companies/Spike_Chunsoft | Franchises=Franchises/Shonen_Jump | Genres=Genres/Fighting | Locations=Locations/Arena,Locations/Kame_House,Locations/Korin_Tower,Locations/Planet_Namek,Locations/Soul_Society | Objects=Objects/Feces,Objects/Glasses | Platforms=Platforms/PlayStation_3,Platforms/PlayStation_Network_Vita,Platforms/PlayStation_Vita | Publishers=Companies/Bandai_Namco_Entertainment | Games=Games/Battle_Stadium_D_O_N,Games/Dragon_Ball_Zenkai_Battle_Royal,Games/Golden_Knight_GARO | Themes=Themes/Anime,Themes/Martial_Arts | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/8/87790/2998761-box_jsvs.png }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"2998761-box_jsvs.png","path":"8\/87790\/","mime":"image\/png","sizes":"square_medium,scale_large,square_mini,screen_medium,scale_super,square_small,scale_small,scale_avatar,square_avatar,scale_medium,square_tiny,scale_tiny,screen_kubrick","caption":null},"background":{"file":"2598986-4288102983-58056.jpg","path":"0\/1749\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"square_tiny,square_medium,screen_kubrick_wide,square_small,square_avatar,screen_medium,scale_large,scale_small,square_mini,scale_super,scale_medium,scale_avatar,screen_kubrick","caption":""}}' /> '''J-Stars Victory VS''' is a 3D Arena Fighting game starring various popular stars of the [[Franchises/Shonen_Jump|Shonen Jump]] manga magazine. Graphically and overall aesthetically, it resembles the arcade title [[Games/Dragon_Ball_Zenkai_Battle_Royal|Dragon Ball Zenkai Battle Royal]]. J-Stars Victory VS is a [[Concepts/Anniversary_Edition|celebratory title]], created for the 45th anniversary of Shonen Jump.
The game's initial reveal showed that [[Characters/Goku|Goku]] ([[Franchises/Dragon_Ball|Dragon Ball]]), [[Characters/Monkey_D_Luffy|Monkey D. Luffy]] ([[Franchises/One_Piece|One Piece]]), and [[Characters/Toriko_25128|Toriko]] ([[Franchises/Toriko|Toriko]]) were playable. This is the first time that Toriko has been included in a [[Concepts/Crossover|crossover]] game. A blurry image in one of the screenshots seemed to indicate that [[Characters/Naruto_Uzumaki|Naruto Uzumaki]] ([[Franchises/Naruto|Naruto]]) was included, and he was confirmed on March 27, 2013.
June 5, 2013, saw confirmation of [[Characters/Himura_Kenshin|Kenshin Himura]] ([[Franchises/Rurouni_Kenshin|Rurouni Kenshin]]) and [[Characters/Ichigo_Kurosaki|Ichigo Kurosaki]] ([[Franchises/Bleach|Bleach]]).
An expanded, English version of the game called [[Games/J_Stars_Victory_Vs|J-Stars Victory Vs+]] will be released in western territories in Summer 2015. This version of the game will feature a new arcade mode and balance changes, and also debuts on the [[Platforms/PlayStation_4|PlayStation 4]] with enhanced graphics.

{{Game | Name=James Cameron's Avatar | Guid=3030-30549 | Deck=This game's story takes place 20 years before the movie, where you take upon a role of Ryan Lorenz. It also involves Neytiri and her sister Sylwanin. | ReleaseDate=2009-12-15 | Characters=Characters/Dr_Grace_Augustine,Characters/John_Cage,Characters/Neytiri,Characters/Ryan_Lorenz,Characters/Sylwanin,Characters/Tsamlok,Characters/TsuHak | Concepts=Concepts/Alien,Concepts/Games_Based_on_Movies,Concepts/Licensed_Game,Concepts/Mount,Concepts/Navi,Concepts/RDA,Concepts/Tribe | Developers=Companies/Gameloft_S_A | Franchises=Franchises/James_Camerons_Avatar | Genres=Genres/Platformer,Genres/Role_Playing,Genres/Shooter | Locations=Locations/Alpha_Centauri,Locations/Pandora_2342 | Objects= | Platforms=Platforms/Android,Platforms/iPad,Platforms/iPhone | Publishers=Companies/Gameloft_S_A | Games= | Themes=Themes/Fantasy,Themes/Sci_Fi | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/3/33745/1309251-avatar_ipod.png }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"1309251-avatar_ipod.png","path":"3\/33745\/","mime":"image\/png","sizes":"square_avatar,scale_small,square_small,scale_avatar,square_tiny,square_medium,scale_medium,screen_medium,scale_large,square_mini,scale_super,scale_tiny,screen_kubrick","caption":"Title Screen"},"background":[]}' />
==Gameplay== The game is played by on-screen joystick.
The gameplay is platforming mixed with RPG elements. There are several quests in the game.
Player can also use 3 weapons:
* Avatar Assault Rifle * Na'vi Staff * Na'vi Bow
==Story== You control [[Characters/Ryan_Lorenz|Ryan Lorenz]], the first Avatar just arrived from Earth. Ryan is given mission by [[Characters/John_Cage|John Cage]] to scout the land for any Unobtanium deposits. Ryan Lorenz got lost during his first mission. He thought he was moving towards Hell's Gate but instead was on his way to Ni'awve clan territory. He decided to rest under a big tree for a night, where he received a vision from Eywa of Pandora's future if the humans are allowed to continue their work. Ryan Lorenz was found by the Ni'awve and brought to heir hometree at Valley of Origins. There he assisted the [[Concepts/Navi|Na'vi]] with their problems and was accepted as one of the people. When the humans started to move to the Ni'awve lands, Ryan Lorenz helped them to drive them back. Once [[Characters/Sylwanin|Sylwanin]] was kidnapped by the humans, Ryan Lorenz infiltrated the base and rescued her. Back at the Ni'awve lands he helped them plan an attack on the Hell's Gate, but his last action of rescue was the final drop in insubordination and was shot on his link unit. Ryan Lorenz was remembered as a hero by all the Na'vi.
The game's story is narrated by Zoe Saldaña as [[Characters/Neytiri|Neytiri]].
==Other Versions== * [[Games/James_Camerons_Avatar_The_Game_30564|Nintendo DS version]] lets you play a Na'vi named [[Characters/Nok|Nok]], from Tawkami clan. * [[Games/James_Camerons_Avatar_The_Game_2009|Nintendo Wii andamp;amp;amp; PSP version]] allows player control Na'vis: [[Characters/Raiuk|Rai'uk]], and [[Characters/Kyuna|Kyuna]] for second player. * [[Games/James_Camerons_Avatar_The_Game|PC, PS3 and X-Box 360 version]] player can control female or male Avatar driver [[Characters/Able_Ryder|"Able" Ryder]]. * [[Games/James_Camerons_Avatar_2009|Mobile version]] let's you play as Avatar driver [[Characters/Jake_Sully|Jake Sully]] himself.

page.title: Games/Janet DeMornay is a Slumlord (and a witch)

revision.comment: Initial creation of the game page for Jigsudo
revision.text:
{{Game |Guid=0b551268-52e5-4843-bcc5-a9b64ead4a82 |Name=Jigsudo |Deck=Jigsudo is a minimalist daily puzzle game that challenges players through six interconnected stages of logic, memory, and pattern recognition |ReleaseDateType=Full |ReleaseDate=2026-04-05 }} Jigsudo is a daily logic puzzle platform designed for browser-based play. The game offers a continuous flow of six interconnected challenges that reset every 24 hours at 06:00 UTC.
Players progress through a structured sequence that begins with a Memory Game to unlock specific grid blocks, followed by a Jigsaw Puzzle to assemble the main board. This board then serves as the foundation for a Sudoku challenge, Peaks & Valleys identification, a numerical Word-Search style task, and finally, a pattern-matching sequence called "The Code."
The game features a persistent ranking system measured in Rank Points (RP), allowing players to climb from Novice to the rank of Eternal. It is built as a Progressive Web App (PWA), ensuring accessibility across multiple devices via web browsers.

{{Game |Guid=5a2351ec-5c07-460b-9473-12859113ab74 |Name=Join Us |Image=https://giantbomb.com/api/media/file/media--file--asdoifjas-06-07-2026.jpg |BackgroundImage=https://giantbomb.com/api/media/file/media--file--poiuqwer-06-07-2026.jpg |Deck=A doomsday cult simulator survival game. |ReleaseDateType=Month |MonthPart=March |MonthYearPart=2027 |Concepts=Concepts/Digital Distribution, Concepts/Polygonal 3D, Concepts/Third Person Perspective, Concepts/Open World, Concepts/Cults, Concepts/Steam, Concepts/Online, Concepts/Cooperative Play, Concepts/Steam Cloud, Concepts/Steam Achievements, Concepts/Achievements |Developers=Companies/Wolf Haus Games |Genres=Genres/Action Adventure, Genres/Simulation |Platforms=Platforms/PC, Platforms/Xbox Series X S, Platforms/PlayStation 5 |Publishers=Companies/Wolf Haus Games |Themes=Themes/Post Apocalyptic }}

revision.comment: Restore image alignment, sizes and captions from legacy source (3030-32826)
revision.text:
{{Game | Name=Jurassic Park: Survival | Guid=3030-32826 | Deck=Planned to coincide with Jurassic Park 3, Survival was to see players take on dinosaurs and terrorists alike as they fought to escape Isla Sorna. The game, however, quickly went extinct. | Characters= | Concepts=Concepts/Cancelled_Games,Concepts/Compsognathus,Concepts/Dinosaur,Concepts/Evil_Organization,Concepts/Mercenary,Concepts/Scientist_5852,Concepts/Survival | Developers=Companies/Savage_Entertainment_LLC | Franchises=Franchises/Jurassic_Park | Genres=Genres/Action_Adventure,Genres/Shooter | Locations=Locations/Isla_Sorna | Objects=Objects/Gun | Platforms=Platforms/PlayStation_2 | Publishers=Companies/Konami,Companies/Universal_Interactive_Inc | Games=Games/Dinosaur_Hunting,Games/Jurassic_The_Hunted,Games/The_Lost_World_Jurassic_Park_4125,Games/Trespasser_Jurassic_Park | Themes=Themes/Prehistoric,Themes/Sci_Fi | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/7/72276/1542554-jurassic_park_survival_logo.gif }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"1542554-jurassic_park_survival_logo.gif","path":"7\/72276\/","mime":"image\/gif","sizes":"square_avatar,square_tiny,scale_small,square_small,square_medium,scale_avatar,square_mini,scale_medium,screen_medium,scale_large,scale_super,scale_tiny,screen_kubrick","caption":""},"background":[]}' />
==Overview== Designed as a showpiece for [[Companies/Savage_Entertainment_LLC|Savage Entertainment]]'s Sabertooth Engine that they hoped to license out to developers, Jurassic Park: Survival was first announced at E3 2001. A joint publishing effort between [[Companies/Universal_Interactive_Inc|Universal Interactive]] and [[Companies/Konami|Konami]], Survival was in development for a Holiday 2001 debut on PlayStation 2 to tie into the DVD release of that Summer's blockbuster film, Jurassic Park 3.
'''''' =='''Story'''== '''''' {{GBFigure|src=http://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_small/7/72276/1542563-bones2.jpg|full=http://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/7/72276/1542563-bones2.jpg|align=right}} Though not directly tied to the plot of Jurassic Park 3, Survival was to be set on Isla Sorna and feature, among other dinosaurs, the Spinosaurus first shown off in the movie. Press materials name the main character as both David Vaughn and David Espinoza but regardless of ethnicity he remains a member of a "distinguished science team" sent to the island on "a mission of research". All that science goes right out the window, however, when David discovers that the security team protecting the research substation is actually a group of hired mercenaries sent by a rival corporation to steal dinosaur DNA. Escaping on a helicopter with fellow scientists, the mercenaries shoot them down and leave them for dead, taking other survivors as hostages.
Things turn [[Franchises/Half_Life|Half-Life]] as David goes from unassuming scientist to expert marksman and survivalist in order to simultaneously deal with dinosaur attacks, stop the escaping rogue agents, round up his kidnapped colleagues and ultimately escape the island intact.
'''''' =='''Gameplay'''== '''''' {{GBFigure|src=http://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_small/7/72276/1542565-jump_2.jpg|full=http://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/7/72276/1542565-jump_2.jpg|align=right}} Rarely seen in playable form the game would've been a third-person shooter with exploration and puzzle-solving elements. David is seen with various handguns and in direct contact with velociraptors and Tyrannosaurs Rex. A hacking feature was promised that would've let players tap into a surveillance network to scope out enemy locations and possibly release dinosaurs from their pens to take out mercenaries. Lots of climbing, rappeling, and block-pushing can been seen in this early [http://www.gamefaqs.com/ps2/480863-jurassic-park-survival/videos/gs_video-2764428 trailer]. Environments in development included jungles, canyons, caverns, and manmade encampments and facilities. A vehicle section was also in the works with players racing a heavily armored ATV across the island, dodging dinosaurs and hazards and battling with the mercenaries. The Sabertooth Engine not only looked promising for a PlayStation 2 product, it was also to enable dinosaurs to realistically bend trees and cause rippling water and even terrain.
==Cancellation== Although Jurassic Park 3 performed well in theaters its general reception seemed midling and its tired plot portrayed by an unexpected cast may have led to the game's ultimate demise. By the time the movie was on DVD Jurassic Park: Survival was long forgotten and supplemented by a handful of Game Boy titles from Konami.
